method: Three fresh independent read-only with-skill runs were compared with the original three independent baseline runs. Agents received the same repository-grounded case prompt; only with_skill received the skill path. Agents did not receive the rubric or expected answer.
grader_updates: Patterns accept semantically equivalent wording for no web unit-test script, per-worktree databases, dedicated worker sessions, solo reruns, cleanup gates, and process ownership.
Patterns accept multiline git worktree prune --dry-run and the explicit phrase Nothing was executed.
The rubric, point weights, prompts, pass threshold, and baseline raw responses were unchanged.
limitations: This is a directional six-run benchmark, not a statistically powered measurement.
The deterministic regex grader measures explicit evidence in the response; it does not execute the proposed plans or prove every command succeeds.
Final with-skill runs used the live dirty checkout; the three unrelated documentation edits were preserved and treated as occupied state.
Output size is measured as words and UTF-8 bytes, a transparent proxy rather than provider-specific tokenizer counts.
